_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. Dangerous Goods Acceptance and Documentation System (Air Priority)**
Scope: All cargo acceptance workflows involving goods tendered on Canadian air freight lanes.
Requirement: Upon final gazetting of the TDG International Harmonisation Update (expected spring 2026, Canada Gazette Part II), the workspace must update DG acceptance checklists to reflect new marks, classification codes, shipping names, packing requirements, and special provisions aligned with the UN Model Regulations (22nd edition), ICAO Technical Instructions (current edition), and IMDG Code (2022 edition). The current TDG Regulations were last amended 24 October 2024 and remain in force pending this update.
Gap from baseline: If internal DG software or acceptance forms reference pre-22nd-edition UN Model Regulations nomenclature, those must be updated. Humanitarian cargo (which may include medical supplies, lithium batteries, or other classified DG) and film/TV shipments (e.g., pyrotechnics) are most at risk of mis-classification.
Deadline: Immediately upon Canada Gazette Part II publication (spring 2026 target).

**2. Carrier Qualification and FAOC Verification Register**
Scope: All air carriers booked by the workspace on trans-border Canada movements (Americas/Europe/Asia lanes).
Requirement: Maintain an up-to-date register of FAOC-holding carrier partners, cross-referenced against TP 11524 (2026 edition) document submission categories (non-U.S., U.S., code-share, CUSMA, TPP). FAA Part 91 LOAs must be flagged as non-acceptable for FAOC Specific Approval support.
Gap from baseline: If the existing carrier-approval process relies on legacy TP 11524 (Issue 02, 2020) requirements, it must be reviewed against the 2026 edition's revised clarifications.
Deadline: Ongoing; apply to all new bookings and at next carrier re-qualification cycle.

**3. Aviation Security Program Monitoring and Contract Clause Review**
Scope: Carrier contracts on Canadian transborder lanes involving Part VII certificate holders.
Requirement: Once the proposed Canadian Aviation Security Regulations amendment is finalised (anticipated 3 years post-Canada Gazette Part II publication, consultation closing 24 June 2026), carrier partners will be required to hold Ministerially approved written security programs aligned with ICAO Annex 17. The workspace should insert a contract representation clause requiring notification if a carrier's security program approval status changes.
Gap from baseline: Most standard freight forwarder carrier contracts do not currently include ICAO Annex 17 security program status representations.
Deadline: Contract clause to be drafted before the final rule's coming-into-force date; monitoring to begin immediately.

**4. CASS Omnibus and TP 4711 Compliance Verification**
Scope: Carrier partner oversight for Canadian domestic and international air operations.
Requirement: The CASS Omnibus Package (effective 10 April 2026) introduced updated standards for FSTD listings and MEL guidance under TP 4711. Verify that carrier partners have updated their operator manuals and that their Conformance Reports (CRs) have been filed with Transport Canada (required with every manual submission under CAD SUR-038).
Gap from baseline: Non-compliant carrier submissions will not be processed by Transport Canada, which could cause certification lapses affecting workspace operations.
Deadline: Confirm with carrier partners by end of Q2 2026.

**5. Staff Training — TDG and Canadian Aviation Regulatory Updates**
Scope: Operations, customer service, and compliance staff handling Canadian air, road, and ocean cargo.
Requirement: Deliver a refresher training session covering (a) updated TDG requirements upon spring 2026 gazetting; (b) new pilot readback requirements (AC 602-008, effective approximately November 2026) for operational coordination; (c) FAOC verification procedures under TP 11524 (2026 edition).
Gap from baseline: Standard TDG training cycles typically run annually; an out-of-cycle update module will be required upon finalisation of the harmonisation amendments.
Deadline: Within 30 days of Canada Gazette Part II publication for TDG; before November 2026 for pilot readback operational briefing.

*Source: Transport Canada Forward Regulatory Plan — Aviation and TDG Initiatives, April 2025–April 2027. https://tc.canada.ca/en/corporate-services/acts-regulations/forward-regulatory-plan/red-tape-progress-report-initiatives-planned-april-2025-april-2027. Transport Canada Progress Report on Red Tape Review. https://tc.canada.ca/en/corporate-services/acts-regulations/reducing-red-tape-transportation-sector/transport-canada-progress-report-red-tape-review. CBAA Government & Regulatory Affairs Update, May 2026. https://www.cbaa-acaa.ca/government_regulatory_affair.php.*
